info.name: Soracom Auth and Access Management API
Performs authentication to access to the Soracom API.

items.docs:
Performs authentication to access to the Soracom API. Please include the following information in your request.

- To perform authentication by a root account, specify `email` and `password`.
- To perform authentication by an AuthKey, specify `authKeyId` and `authKey`.
- To perform authentication by a SAM user, specify `operatorId`, `userName` and `password`.

An API key and API token will be included in the response if successful. Use the API key and API token to perform subsequent requests.

info.name: Revokes an API key and API token in order to remove access to the Soracom API.

items.docs: Revokes the API key and API token that was used to call this API. Once revoked, the API key and API token cannot be used to make further requests to the API, regardless of the key and token's original expiration time. This API can be called regardless of any permission settings.

info.name: Issues a password reset token for the operator.

items.docs: Generates a password reset token and sends it to the operator's primary email address. After receiving the password reset token, call /v1/auth/password_reset_token/verify API with the token to update the operator's password.

info.name: Verifies the password reset token and updates password.

items.docs: Updates the operator's password if the password reset token is verified.

info.name: Generates an API key and an API token to call APIs as a different user.

items.docs: Using the API key and API token of the original user, call this API to issue an API key and API token of the target user that you are switching to. The original user should already be registered in the target user's trust policy. See [User:updateUserTrustPolicy](#!/User/updateUserTrustPolicy) for information on updating the trust policy.
Returns information about the operator.
Returns the operator's AuthKey list.
Generates an AuthKey for the operator.
Deletes an AuthKey from the operator.
Gets the operator's company information.
Creates the operator's company information.
Updates the operator's company information.

info.name: Get operator configuration of the namespace

items.docs: Gets operator configuration of the namespace.

info.name: Set operator configuration of the namespace

items.docs: Sets operator configuration of the namespace.

info.name: Delete operator configuration of the namespace

items.docs: Deletes operator configuration of the namespace.
Adds the operator's contract.
Deletes the operator's contract.
Adds the operator's coverage type.
Gets the operator's individual information.
Creates the operator's individual information.
Updates the operator's individual information.
Gets operator's MFA status. The MFA status is one of `ACTIVE`, `INACTIVE` or `UNCONFIRMED`.
Enables operator's MFA. After calling this API, it should be verified by calling `Operator:verifyMFA` API.
Revokes operator's MFA without backup codes.

Verifies operator's MFA with OTP code. This API must be called after calling `Operator:enableMFA`. MFA will not be activated unless the MFA OTP is verified with this API. Be sure to securely store the backup code returned in the response to this API.
Updates the operator's password.
Returns a token for accessing the support console.

Generates a new API token. If you insert the current API token into the header and make a request, a response is returned containing the new API token. You can then use the new API token in future requests.

info.name: Revoke all API keys and API tokens that were generated by the root user

items.docs: Revoke all API keys and API tokens that were generated by the root user. Once revoked, the API key and API token cannot be used to call the Soracom API, regardless of their expiration time.
Issues a token to revoke operator's MFA. The issued token will be sent to the operator via email.

Verifies the one-time token which is previously issued by calling `/operators/mfa_revoke_token/issue` API, operator's email address, password, and one of the backup codes. If verified, operator's MFA is going to be revoked.

Retrieves whether the SAM user has a password or not.

info.name: Revoke all API keys and API tokens that were generated by the specified SAM user.

items.docs: Revoke all API keys and API tokens that were generated by the specified SAM user. Once revoked, the API key and API token cannot be used to call the SORACOM API, regardless of their expiration time.
Gets user trust policy for switching user.

Updates the trust policy of the user specified by `operator_id` and `user_name` parameters.

**Warning**: Setting a trust policy will allow the operator(s) or user(s) specified in the request body to switch to this SAM user. When switching, the trusted operator(s) or user(s) will be granted the same permissions as this SAM user, and may be able to see session history, traffic history, and other account information.
Deletes user trust policy for switching user.
Get the default permissions rule that is applied to all of the SAM users
Update the default permissions rule that is applied to all of the SAM
Delete the default permissions rule that is applied to all of the SAM

params.name: owner
params.description:
Filters by the type of the role. Specify one of the following:
- `operator`: Operator managed role (default)
- `soracom`: Soracom managed role
- `all`: All roles

items.docs: Returns a list of roles.

params.name: role_id
params.description: Role ID. For operator managed roles, specify the role ID provided during role creation. For Soracom managed roles, specify in SRN (Soracom Resource Name) format.

items.docs: Retrieves a role.
Adds a new operator managed role.
Edits an operator managed role.
Deletes an operator managed role.

params.name: role_id
params.description: Role ID. For operator managed roles, specify the role ID provided during role creation. For Soracom managed roles, specify in SRN (Soracom Resource Name) format.

items.docs: Retrieves a list of users attached to a role.

params.name: role_id
params.description: SRN (Soracom Resource Name) of the Soracom managed role. The format is `srn:soracom:::Role:[roleName]`.

items.docs: Retrieves a list of versions for a Soracom managed role.
Retrieves a list of the user's roles.
Attaches a role to a user.

params.name: role_id
params.description: Role ID. For operator managed roles, specify the role ID provided during role creation. For Soracom managed roles, specify in SRN (Soracom Resource Name) format.

items.docs: Detaches a role from a user.
Retrieves a list of email addresses registered to the operator.
Retrieves information for the email address corresponding to the specified email address ID.
Deletes the email address corresponding to the specified email address ID from the operator.

info.name: Issue an authentication token to add an email address

items.docs:
Generates an authentication token for adding a new email address and sends it to the specified email address. This authentication token is used with [Email:verifyAddEmailToken API](#/Email/verifyAddEmailToken) to complete email address verification and addition.

**Warning**: This API can only be executed with root user credentials.

info.name: Verify an authentication token to add an email address

items.docs:
Verifies the authentication token issued by [Email:issueAddEmailToken API](#/Email/issueAddEmailToken) and adds the email address to the operator.

**Warning**: Authentication tokens have an expiration time. Expired authentication tokens cannot be used.
Returns a list of email settings.
Return information on email settings.

info.name: Set an email address in the email settings

items.docs: Set an email address in the email settings.

info.name: Delete an email address from the email settings

items.docs: Delete an email address from the email settings.
